# Shoulders

> An AI workspace for researchers. Writing, reference management, coding, and AI assistance — designed as one system. Local-first, open source, multi-format.

Shoulders is a desktop application that integrates text editing, reference management, code execution, and AI assistance for researchers. It handles Markdown (with PDF export via Typst), LaTeX (via Tectonic), Word documents (.docx), Jupyter notebooks, and code files in a single workspace. Built on Tauri v2 (Rust + Vue 3), all files remain on the researcher's machine.

## Docs

- [Getting Started](https://shoulde.rs/docs/getting-started.md): Download Shoulders, open a folder, and start writing.
- [Markdown](https://shoulde.rs/docs/markdown.md): Write in Markdown with formatting shortcuts, wiki links, preview, and PDF export.
- [Word Documents](https://shoulde.rs/docs/word.md): Open and edit .docx files natively, with tracked changes and AI support.
- [LaTeX](https://shoulde.rs/docs/latex.md): Write and compile LaTeX documents with live preview, error navigation, and AI assistance.
- [References & Citations](https://shoulde.rs/docs/references.md): Import references, cite them in your documents, and export bibliographies.
- [Setup](https://shoulde.rs/docs/ai-setup.md): Configure AI access, models, privacy controls, and project-specific instructions.
- [Inline Suggestions](https://shoulde.rs/docs/inline-suggestions.md): AI-powered text completions that appear as ghost text at your cursor.
- [AI Chat](https://shoulde.rs/docs/ai-chat.md): A conversation with an AI that can read, search, and edit your workspace.
- [Document Comments](https://shoulde.rs/docs/ai-comments.md): Annotate your documents with comments, then submit them to the AI for revision.
- [Tools & Review](https://shoulde.rs/docs/ai-tools.md): What the AI can do in your workspace, and how to review its changes.
- [Workflows](https://shoulde.rs/docs/workflows.md): Structured AI pipelines that run inside Shoulders.
- [Tool API](https://shoulde.rs/docs/tool-api.md): Use Claude Code or other CLI tools with full access to your workspace.
- [Code & Notebooks](https://shoulde.rs/docs/code.md): Run R, Python, and Julia code alongside your writing. Edit Jupyter notebooks natively.
- [Navigation & Settings](https://shoulde.rs/docs/navigation.md): Quick Open, the file tree, version history, themes, and workspace configuration.
- [Keyboard Shortcuts](https://shoulde.rs/docs/shortcuts.md): Complete reference. On Windows and Linux, use Ctrl instead of Cmd and Alt instead of Option.
- [Privacy & Data](https://shoulde.rs/docs/privacy.md): Where your data lives, what leaves your machine, and how to control it.

## Full documentation

- [Documentation index](https://shoulde.rs/docs/index.md): Table of contents with links to each section as markdown
- [Complete docs in plain text](https://shoulde.rs/llms-full.txt): All documentation in a single file, suitable for LLM context

## About

- [About Shoulders](https://shoulde.rs/about.md): Mission and philosophy — AI research tools built with intention, preserving researcher agency
- [Pricing](https://shoulde.rs/pricing.md): Free with own API keys. Shoulders accounts include free AI usage during research preview.
- [Download](https://shoulde.rs/download.md): Desktop application for macOS
- [Enterprise](https://shoulde.rs/enterprise.md): Managed deployment, data governance, site licenses
- [GitHub](https://github.com/shoulders-ai/shoulders): Source code

## Optional

- [Terms of Service](https://shoulde.rs/terms.md)
- [Privacy Policy](https://shoulde.rs/privacy.md)
